MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Lee, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.
Stephen J. Healy, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Dungy

RULING:
DEFENDANT MILWAUKEE VALVE COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong discussed in Aguilar with regard to plaintiff being in the vicinity of valves attributable to defendant. of Defendant's separate statement failed to contain sufficient facts that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the valves attributable to defendant that plaintiff was exposed to did not contain asbestos. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
